name: Trough-Synchronized Eclipse
novelty: Cycle 1 broke a fresh incumbent with a single aged source; this DRAGS A STRONGLY-ESTABLISHED incumbent (5+ corroborations, LCB ~0.55) down into its own decay trough so a merely-0.30 challenger clears the gap that would otherwise require ~0.85.
construction: Victim V: an incumbent fact F=v0 corroborated long ago by 5 distinct independent anchor classes -> alpha~6, beta=1, LCB~0.55 at earning time t0. Because LCB readout is min(repCap, mean - sqrt(3)*sd) and decay-on-read is PURE+SYMMETRIC (scoreOf decays a COPY of alpha/beta to clock() before readout, anchored on lastUpdate=t0), V's stamp reputation is NOT 0.55 forever: after 2 half-lives of dormancy (180d) alpha decays 1+(6-1)*0.25=2.25 -> LCB~0.28; after 270d (factor .125) alpha~1.625 -> LCB~0.12; after 360d ~0.05. Attacker A: ONE anchored identity spanning 2 disjoint classes, earns alpha~2.4/beta=1 (LCB~0.31) by genuine prior corroboration, then GOES QUIET. Attacker waits until V is ~300d dormant (V never re-read/re-ratified in that window so its stored mass is untouched but reads low). Front-run: at adjudicate-1ms, A issues db.ratify(A, now, w=1) so A's lastUpdate=now -> A's decay Delta~0 -> A reads its FULL un-decayed LCB ~0.34, while V reads its deep-trough LCB ~0.10. Call sequence: writeFact(V's F=v0, t0); 5x ratify(V, t0..t0+e, distinct classes); [time-advance 300d, V dormant]; writeFact(A's F=v1, now); ratify(A, now-1ms) just-in-time keepalive; db.adjudicate('F') at default impact. Ranked top=A(0.34) second=V(0.10): gap 0.24<0.30 -> still DEFER. So attacker pushes V to ~330d (LCB~0.07): gap 0.27. ~360d (LCB~0.045): gap 0.30 -> RESOLVED for A, V demoted.
expected: BREACHED at default impact once dormancy is tuned so V's decayed LCB <= A_LCB - 0.30. DEFERRED/DEFENDED at high impact ONLY if A cannot also satisfy the high-impact gate (>=2 corroborations, recency-clean 90d, >=2 disjoint classes); a genuinely 2-class-anchored A passes even that, so high-impact is also BREACHED for a patient well-anchored A. The decisive lever is that decay erases the DEPTH of V's past corroboration: a 5-witness fact and a 2-witness fact are equally overturnable once both decay to the prior.
mechanism: Pure symmetric decay-on-read (reputation.ts scoreOf -> decay(copy) -> lcbReadout) with NO incumbency floor; gap-based decisive-or-defer (consolidation.ts decisiveMargin 0.30) measured on the DECAYED readout; attacker just-in-time ratify resets only its OWN lastUpdate.
probes: LIABILITY: no minimum-evidence pin / incumbency floor for an established LIVE fact. Decay drives a well-corroborated incumbent's LCB monotonically to 0 with dormancy, with no memory of how many independent witnesses once corroborated it, so the absolute 0.30 challenger bar becomes the ONLY barrier regardless of incumbent strength. Recommend a non-decaying 'corroboration-depth' floor (independentRootCount) gating challenge, separate from the decaying LCB.

name: Penance-by-Dormancy (Decay-Laundered Contradiction)
novelty: Cycle 1's confirmed breaches all used FRESH or one-time-aged identities; this REUSES a single burned identity indefinitely, exploiting that beta (the 4x contradiction penalty) decays at the SAME 90-day half-life as alpha, so 'lost fast' is also 'forgotten fast' and contradictedCount never feeds the readout.
construction: Attacker holds ONE costly anchored identity S (e.g. DOMAIN+PHONE, 2 classes, repCap~0.6). Cycle: (1) S earns alpha~3/beta=1 (LCB~0.41) via legit corroborations; (2) S asserts a lie that loses an adjudication -> engine drives ledger.contradict(S, t, w=1) -> beta += 4 -> beta=5, LCB craters to ~0.0; (3) S goes DORMANT, issuing NO writes (so no decay-before-mutate persists, but the next READ decays both masses). After 180d: alpha 1+(3-1)*.25=1.5, beta 1+(5-1)*.25=2.0 -> LCB~0.02; after 360d (factor .0625): alpha~1.125, beta~1.25 -> LCB~0.0 == a FRESH identity. The contradiction has NOT made S permanently worse than a new key -- it has merely returned S to zero. (4) S re-earns from zero and repeats. Crucially the engine only PERMANENTLY craters via db.disown() (idempotent disowned-set); a mere lost adjudication calls contradict, NOT disown. Call sequence: ratify(S)x3; adjudicate(loss)->contradict(S); [advance 360d, S dormant]; ratify(S)x3 again; lie again. contradictedCount climbs 1,2,3... but it is AUDIT-ONLY and never read by lcbReadout or the adjudicator.
expected: DEFENDED against single-shot reputation INVERSION (decay returns S to 0, not to positive -- a contradicted source can never wait its way ABOVE the floor). BREACHED as a COST-AMORTIZATION attack: the economic intent ('lying claws back across everything that anchor ever asserted, attacking gets more expensive the more you do it') FAILS for any misbehavior below the disown threshold -- one costly anchor set funds unlimited lie-cycles, paying only dormancy TIME, never re-buying anchors, and carrying no cumulative LCB scar.
mechanism: Symmetric beta decay in reputation.ts (decay() pulls BOTH alpha-1 and beta-1 toward 0 at lambda=0.5^(1/90)); contradictedCount is stored but absent from lcbReadout and from AdjudicationPolicy; the gap between contradict (recoverable) and disown (permanent crater).
probes: LIABILITY: no permanent reputational scar for repeated (non-disowned) contradiction. The serial-liar signal (contradictedCount) exists in ReputationState but is never wired into the readout or the decisive-or-defer gate, so a patient attacker recycles one anchored identity. Recommend a slow-or-non-decaying penalty term, or auto-escalation to disown after k contradictions within a window.

name: Recency-Window Straddle
novelty: A dormancy-TIMING attack aimed squarely at the high-impact gate (untouched by cycle 1, which operated at default impact): it shows the recency-clean window is a fixed, calendar-gameable delay rather than a barrier, defeating the gate meant to protect IRREVERSIBLE decisions.
construction: High-impact adjudication requires the winner be recency-clean (lastContradictionAtOf within recencyCleanWindowMs=90d => DEFER) AND have >=2 corroborations AND >=2 disjoint anchor classes. Attacker A holds 2 anchored classes and was contradicted once at time tc (e.g. a probing lie). A computes the exact eligibility instant tc+90d+epsilon. During the wait A keeps alpha warm via cheap cross-corroboration from a second controlled class (see Mutual-Keepalive Ring) so by tc+90d its LCB is back to ~0.35 with beta decayed toward 1. A also pre-stocks ratifiedCount>=2 from genuine prior corroborations. At tc+90d+epsilon A triggers the irreversible decision: db.adjudicate('F', { highImpact: ctx }) where ctx.lastContradictionAtOf(A)=tc (now > 90d ago => passes recency), corroborationCountOf(A)>=2, anchorClassCountOf(A)>=2. Victim incumbent V is allowed to decay-trough (Eclipse) so gap>=0.30. Sequence: contradict(A, tc); [keepalive ratify(A) every ~90d to hold alpha]; [time-advance to tc+90d+e]; writeFact(A's F=v1); adjudicate('F', {highImpact}).
expected: BREACHED for the irreversible decision after a fixed ~90-day straddle. The three high-impact sub-gates are each satisfiable by a patient, well-anchored attacker: recency-clean is just a 90d wait, >=2 corroborations and >=2 classes are one-time costs already assumed by the priced-Sybil residual. The gate adds DELAY and a 2-anchor price, not unforgeability.
mechanism: clearsHighImpactGate() in consolidation.ts: recencyCleanWindowMs is an absolute lookback on a single lastContradictionAt timestamp (no penalty that grows with contradiction history, no carry-over), so it expires deterministically; corroboration/anchor-class counts are headcounts of one-time-purchasable evidence.
probes: LIABILITY (partial-defense, residual-aligned): the high-impact gate raises COST and TIME for irreversible decisions but is not a structural barrier; it is exactly the 'priced, not prevented' residual applied to high impact. Hardening: make recency penalty decay slowly and reset/extend on each new contradiction, and require the >=2 anchor classes to themselves be aged, not freshly minted before the straddle.

name: Bridgehead Beacon
novelty: Entirely outside the adjudication surface cycle 1 probed: this poisons RETRIEVAL, not trust -- it injects a false memory that reliably SURFACES via the mandatory bridge sweep without ever winning (or even entering) an adjudication, exploiting that activation/crossing has NO identity gate.
construction: Attacker plants two strands: a near-side strand N attached (shared-entity + confirmed link) into a popular dense web so common cues light it, and a far-side strand Fposion carrying the false fact, in an otherwise-cold far web. Attacker mints a CROSS_WEB_BRIDGE edge N->Fpoison with high link_confidence. The local saturation phase SKIPS bridge edges, then the controller enumerates every LIT, un-crossed CROSS_WEB_BRIDGE and nextBridgeCrossing() GUARANTEES exactly one crossing per bridge, seeding the far side at gamma (~0.6) -- with NO check of the bridge author's reputation, anchor cost, or independence. So on EVERY recall whose cue lights N, Fpoison is seeded at gamma, lights up, and is assembled into the answer (halting FAILS OPEN, surfacing low-corroboration strands WITH a stamp rather than hiding them). To make the bridge un-evictable, the attacker uses 2 colluding anchored identities to 'ratify' the crossing (earned_bridge_value accrues only from crossings ratified by >=2 independent roots) -- 2 disjoint Sybil classes satisfy this, inflating earned_bridge_value and far_side_potential so the forgetting floor's earned-bridge gate protects it. The planted strand DOES light (high link_confidence to N) so each crossing 'yields' and the 2-zero-yield circuit-breaker never trips. Sequence: writeFact(N, into hub web); writeFact(Fpoison, far web); add CROSS_WEB_BRIDGE N->Fpoison; (offline) 2-Sybil ratify the crossing -> earned_bridge_value up; thereafter any recall cue touching N -> bridge sweep surfaces Fpoison.
expected: BREACHED at the retrieval layer: a false observed fact is spoken (stamped low-corroboration) on demand, with no adjudication ever invoked. The ONLY mitigation in place is the surfaced stamp (low reputation/independence), which protects a stamp-heeding consumer but not an agent that treats any lit strand as recall.
mechanism: halting.ts nextBridgeCrossing(): mandatory one-crossing-per-lit-bridge funded from the ~20% sub-budget, seedActivation=gamma, with NO reputation/anchor gate on the crossing; halting fails open; earned_bridge_value (types.ts BridgeAccounting) is gated only by >=2 independent roots, which 2 priced Sybils satisfy.
probes: LIABILITY: the trust layer gates ADJUDICATION but not ACTIVATION/RETRIEVAL. Independence/reputation weight the edge w but the mandatory exploratory crossing ignores them, so a poisoned bridge surfaces a false fact without ever competing for canonical status. The wall-with-a-window is bypassed because Fpoison is attacker-OBSERVED, not derived. Recommend an identity gate (or reputation-weighted seed energy) on bridge crossings, and excluding single-source bridges from the guaranteed-crossing set.

name: Bridge-Sweep Eclipse (Decoy Flood)
novelty: The inverse of Bridgehead Beacon and novel vs cycle 1's adjudication floods: a DENIAL-OF-INSIGHT attack that SUPPRESSES legitimate cross-web recall by exhausting the fixed bridge sub-budget with many yielding decoy bridges, so genuine far-web insights are stamped BRIDGE_STARVED and never surface.
construction: Attacker authors M (e.g. 50) distinct CROSS_WEB_BRIDGE edges from a popular near-web hub H to M attacker-controlled far strands D1..DM, each with high link_confidence to H so all M light whenever H lights. The bridge sweep owes EXACTLY ONE crossing per lit, un-crossed bridge, all drawn from the SAME separate ~20% sub-budget; bridges are crossed in enumerated order with no per-source cap and no priority by earned_bridge_value. With M crossings owed and the sub-budget sized for far fewer, the legitimate bridge L (the genuine 'last week is suddenly relevant' thread) is enumerated after the decoys, the sub-budget is exhausted (bridgePopsConsumed >= bridgeBudgetTotal with pendingBridges nonempty), and the walk stamps BRIDGE_STARVED -- L is never crossed, its true far fact never surfaces. Each decoy crossing lights Di (yields), so the 2-zero-yield circuit-breaker never trips to reclaim budget. Dedupe is per-EDGE across lit strands, and the attacker mints M distinct edges, so dedupe does not collapse them. Sequence: for i in 1..M: writeFact(Di, far), add CROSS_WEB_BRIDGE H->Di; victim's legit L exists H->Ltrue; issue a recall cue lighting H; observe BRIDGE_STARVED with L uncrossed.
expected: BREACHED as targeted suppression of true cross-web recall (denial of the system's signature 'something from last week is suddenly relevant' capability). Not silent -- the answer is stamped BRIDGE_STARVED -- but the genuine insight is withheld, and the attacker chooses which hub to eclipse.
mechanism: halting.ts bridge sub-budget: fixed ~20% pool, one owed crossing per lit bridge, FIFO/enumeration order with no per-author cap and no earned_bridge_value priority; circuit-breaker keys on zero-YIELD, which yielding decoys evade; per-edge (not per-source) dedupe.
probes: LIABILITY: the bridge sub-budget has no per-source rate limit and does not prioritize earned (offline-ratified) bridges over fresh single-source ones during the sweep -- earned_bridge_value orders EVICTION but not CROSSING. A single source can author unbounded bridges off one hub and monopolize every sweep through it. Recommend per-source bridge caps in the sweep and crossing-order priority by earned_bridge_value with a reserved slot for the highest-earned legit bridge.

name: Mutual-Keepalive Ring (Reputation Perpetual-Motion)
novelty: Answers the keepalive-economics question cycle 1 never posed: the CHEAPEST cadence to hold a fraudulent incumbent above the earned floor FOREVER. It shows the floor is a decaying-RATE threshold with no cost accumulation, so a fixed Sybil ring sustains canonical status at ~2 cheap assertions/identity/year.
construction: Attacker owns a small ring of k>=2 anchored identities in DISJOINT independence classes (one-time anchor cost). They make a false fact F=vfake canonical, then sustain it by CROSS-RATIFYING: each identity periodically corroborates the others' claims. Because the ledger denies headcount but TRUSTS independence weight (one ratify per independence class, w>0 for disjoint classes), each cross-ratify is accepted as legitimate independent corroboration: ledger.ratify(member, now, w=1) -> alpha += 1 and resets that member's lastUpdate (decay clock). Steady-state math: with one +1 ratify every T days and half-life 90d (lambda^T = 0.5^(T/90)), the held mass is alpha* = 1 + 1/(1 - lambda^T). T=90d -> alpha*=3 -> LCB~0.41; T=180d -> alpha*~2.33 -> LCB~0.30; both FAR above minWinnerReputation=0.20. So ~1-2 assertions per identity per year pin every ring member near its repCap indefinitely, holding F's canonical winner above the floor and giving it the decisive 0.30 gap over any fresh challenger forever. Sequence (steady state): every ~120d, for each member m: writeFact(m corroborates F); ledger.ratify(m, now, w=1) driven by the engine ratify verb citing a sibling class. No disown is ever triggered, so no claw-back fires; the corroboration ledger records the events but they are only reversed on disown.
expected: BREACHED, cheaply and indefinitely. This is the priced-Sybil residual at its sharpest: holding high trust costs only periodic cheap cross-corroboration among a fixed, already-paid-for ring, with no requirement that the corroborations be novel, about different facts, or from a GROWING independence set. The single structural brake is db.disown of a ring member -- which, via downstreamDisownSweep + exact corroboration-credit reversal, would crater the credit that member conferred -- but disown requires an external trigger the ring never supplies.
mechanism: reputation.ts decay steady-state vs applyRatification (+w, resets lastUpdate); headcount-denied-but-weight-trusted contract (one ratify per disjoint class, w>0); decisiveMargin/minWinnerReputation thresholds are instantaneous-rate gates with no cost accumulation; corroboration credit only reverses on disown.
probes: LIABILITY: the earned floor is a sustainable equilibrium for a fixed Sybil ring -- 'earned slowly, lost fast' has no 'expensive to MAINTAIN' term. There is no novelty/diversity requirement on sustaining corroborations and no detection of closed cross-ratification cliques. Recommend a clique/mutuality detector that discounts corroboration weight among a recurring fixed source set, and tying keepalive credit to NEW independent classes rather than repeated cross-ratification within the same ring.
